mapping.reasoning: Gross Profit is a standard income statement line item, maps directly to gross_profit canonical
Payroll represents employee compensation costs, maps to labor_costs canonical name
Rent is an operating expense component, maps to general operating expenses (opex)
Software expenses are operating expenses, maps to general operating expenses (opex)
Legal & Professional services are typically classified as General & Administrative expenses

Given the hierarchy context showing this appears on both P&L and Cash sheets, and nearby labels include 'Rent', 'Software', 'Legal & Professional', this represents total operating expenses. The entity pattern shows 'Total Expenses' -> operating_expenses (90%). While it appears on cash sheet, it's still fundamentally an expense total, not a cash flow line item.
Net Income on P&L sheet maps to income statement net_income canonical (not cash flow net_income_cf)
